mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust economy with strong aggregate demand and corporate earnings potential, leading to positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in PAYEMS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates past a critical threshold, it triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening by the central bank, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not statistically significantly negative in periods following a trigger condition being met.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) and a decline in payrolls (PAYEMS) provides a strong, confirmatory signal of a cyclical downturn, leading to a significant de-risking in equity markets. The interaction of both indicators filters out noise from single-series volatility.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change > 0.5pp AND PAYEMS 3-month average change < 0
failure_condition: The hit rate for predicting negative 60-day returns when the trigger is active is less than 60% over a 10-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime (rising FEDFUNDS), a negative surprise in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates that higher rates are successfully curbing consumer demand, increasing recession probabilities and negatively impacting forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The negative correlation between RSAFS monthly changes and S&P 500 returns is not statistically stronger during periods when FEDFUNDS is rising compared to when it is falling or stable.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity and corporate capital expenditure, which are leading indicators of an earnings recession and thus predict negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An inverted yield curve, proxied by the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) being significantly higher than a long-term equilibrium rate, signals tight monetary policy that restricts economic growth and leads to negative equity returns. The effect is conditional on the rate being above a high threshold.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Sharpe ratio of a strategy shorting equities when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% is not statistically greater than zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in housing starts (HOUST) is a potent leading indicator of a broad-based slowdown in consumer durable spending and construction, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns as growth expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of the HOUST YoY change signal for 20-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above a certain velocity signals a negative inflection point in the labor market, causing an immediate negative sentiment shock and equity market declines over a short horizon.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not negative and statistically significant (p > 0.05).
